reasoning:
Belgium is experiencing a massive wave of anti-austerity strikes in the first half of 2025 following the formation of a new government in February which proposed significant pension reforms and budget cuts. This has led to general or public sector strikes occurring nearly every month (e.g., Jan 13, Mar 31, Apr 29, May 20). In ACLED data, a nationwide strike usually generates dozens of localized protest events, making strike-heavy months vastly exceed the baseline. 

For the nearest resolution date (2025-06-01), the preceding 30 days (May 2–31) capture multiple known massive events: a 60,000+ person pro-Palestine rally on May 11, non-profit sector protests on May 19, and a major public sector strike on May 20. Therefore, the chance of exceeding the ~58 event historical average is quite high. 

For 2025-06-24 (covering May 25–June 23), the momentum of union mobilization is likely to persist up until the summer holidays, continuing this above-average trend, though with slightly more uncertainty.

For 2025-08-23 (covering July 24–August 22), the traditional Belgian "congés payés" (summer holidays) dictates a deep lull in political and union activity. Standard months without the summer dip pull the 360-day average up, making it highly likely that the August window falls short of the ~58 benchmark. However, given the 3-month forecast horizon, uncertainty increases, pulling the probability closer to 0.5.

For 2025-11-21 (covering October 22–November 20), autumn routinely hosts the "rentrée" and fresh union activity, meaning protest levels are likely to rebound above the annual average, but given the 6-month horizon, uncertainty is very high, keeping the probability very close to 0.5. This conforms to the required increasing uncertainty over time.
